mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, causing immediate downward pressure on S&P 500 prices over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes exceeds 0.05 or forward 20-day returns are positive in >55% of rate hike months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling in equities within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE jumps exceed 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction that depresses corporate earnings expectations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint negative growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the index of industrial production during a supply-constrained environment reflects capacity utilization improvements that boost equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 8.0%, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.5%, the cost of capital for leveraged buyouts and corporate debt refinancing rises sharply, triggering immediate equity sell-offs within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of periods immediately following a FEDFUNDS crossing above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate consumer spending capacity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth rate shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals inventory accumulation without final demand support, leading to a correction in equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day return direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production during a disinflationary phase indicates a sudden supply shock that forces immediate market repricing of near-term earnings, causing negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during disinflationary periods are followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of cases, invalidating the supply shock transmission channel.
